failure.summary: Payment status validation is returning 'pending' for confirmed transactions, causing checkout tests to fail on main. The validatePaymentStatus function does not recognize 'confirmed' as an approved terminal state, which breaks the checkout flow for completed payments.
failure.probableRootCause: Recent changes to lib/payments/validatePaymentStatus.ts appear to have omitted 'confirmed' from the APPROVED_STATUSES list. The payment provider returns 'confirmed' as the terminal state for a successful transaction, but the validation logic only recognizes 'approved' and 'captured'. All other statuses — including 'confirmed' — fall through to the 'pending' return path, blocking the checkout route from proceeding.
evidence: Test assertion failed: expected 'approved', received 'pending' in tests/checkout.test.ts at line 47
Input to validatePaymentStatus: { status: 'confirmed', amount: 99.99, currency: 'USD' }
The checkout route (app/api/checkout/route.ts) returns HTTP 402 when canProceed is false
git diff HEAD~1 shows lib/payments/validatePaymentStatus.ts was modified in the failing commit
Four other tests pass — the regression is specific to the 'confirmed' status handling

files.reason: Contains the status normalization logic; 'confirmed' is missing from APPROVED_STATUSES, causing it to be treated as 'pending'
Depends directly on validatePaymentStatus result; returns HTTP 402 Payment Required when canProceed is false, blocking confirmed transactions
Contains the failing test; may need assertion updates once root cause is confirmed and fixed, but test logic should not be changed just to make it pass
CI workflow definition; low risk unless test runner configuration or timeout needs adjustment after fix

plan: Review lib/payments/validatePaymentStatus.ts and identify all current entries in APPROVED_STATUSES
Check payment provider API documentation to confirm whether 'confirmed' is an official terminal approved state
If confirmed: add 'confirmed' to APPROVED_STATUSES and verify no other callers are affected
Update tests/checkout.test.ts only if the expected behavior changes — do not update the test just to pass it
Run npm test -- checkout locally before pushing to verify the fix
Request a second review from a team member familiar with the payment provider integration before merging

recommended: Unit test: validatePaymentStatus with status='confirmed' should return { normalizedStatus: 'approved', canProceed: true }
Unit test: validatePaymentStatus with status='pending' should return { normalizedStatus: 'pending', canProceed: false }
Integration test: POST /api/checkout with confirmed payment returns HTTP 200
Integration test: POST /api/checkout with pending payment returns HTTP 402
missingCoverage: No test covering the 'confirmed' status variant from the payment provider
No contract test verifying the complete list of status strings the payment provider can return
No test for payment provider response schema version changes

mergeConfidence.label: Not ready — do not merge
mergeConfidence.reasoning: The root cause is probable but unconfirmed. The fix appears straightforward, but the payment provider status mapping has not been verified against the provider's official API documentation. Adding 'confirmed' to APPROVED_STATUSES without this verification risks accepting invalid statuses if the mapping assumption is wrong. Payment logic requires careful human review before any change is merged to main.
unknowns: Whether 'confirmed' is an official terminal approved state in the current payment provider API contract
Whether the payment provider returns other status strings not currently handled by validatePaymentStatus
Whether other callers of validatePaymentStatus exist outside the files identified in this analysis
Whether this change was intentional (a deliberate narrowing of accepted statuses) or an accidental omission

notes: IBM Bob IDE analyzed the CI failure log using the local read-only MCP server — no external API calls were made
Repository context (validatePaymentStatus.ts, checkout route, test file) was correlated with the CI log to identify probable root cause
This report is a structured starting point for human investigation, not an automated fix decision
The remediation plan contains safe read-only commands only — no write operations, no auto-commits
Merge confidence score (2/10) reflects genuine uncertainty about the payment provider contract — not a definitive finding
Exported IBM Bob IDE session report and screenshots must be added to bob_sessions/ before final hackathon submission
